ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'bubble tea' class (FOODON_03311737); no specific subclass exists for no-milk, less-sugar, no-topping variant. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific code for flavoured bubble tea without milk. Closest parent match used and documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for flavoured bubble tea beverage variant. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: tea base or fruit flavouring syrup

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving is one medium cup of approximately 500 ml (500 g), as commonly sold at Singaporean bubble tea outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured bubble tea no milk (less sugar, no topping) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Flavoured bubble tea no milk (less sugar, no topping)', CR ID D08010025. All per-100g values are as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — sugar-sweetened fruit-flavoured beverages
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific flavoured bubble tea variant. GI of 62 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sugar-sweetened fruit-flavoured beverages (sucrose-based, ~6g sugar/100ml), which is the closest approved analogous food. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 62 × 31.05 / 100 ≈ 19.3 (based on 500g serving with 6.21g carbohydrate/100g = 31.05g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 6.21g sugar/100g; sodium is negligible at 1mg/100g but dietary sugar independently elevates blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this beverage as its 6.21g sugar/100g content qualifies it as a sugar-sweetened beverage, which independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines; request 0% sugar level to reduce risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 6.21g sugar/100g, zero dietary fibre, and medium GI (estimated 62); liquid sugar is rapidly absorbed causing acute glycaemic excursions
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage as it contains 6.21g free sugar per 100g with no fibre to blunt absorption, resulting in rapid glycaemic impact; a 500ml serving delivers approximately 120 kcal and 31g of free sugar with a glycaemic load of ~19, which is clinically significant per ADA dietary guidelines.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Free sugar content (6.21g/100g) with zero fat and negligible sodium; regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased CVD risk via triglyceride elevation and insulin resistance
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this beverage due to its free sugar content; while fat and sodium are negligible, habitual sugar-sweetened beverage consumption is independently associated with elevated triglycerides and increased CVD risk per AHA dietary guidance; request 0% sugar to make this beverage CVD-safe.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory free sugar load (6.21g/100g) with no anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit in this formulation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage as its 6.21g sugar/100g with zero fibre provides a pro-inflammatory sugar load and no anti-inflammatory benefit; unsweetened green or black tea is preferred for polyphenol intake relevant to RA management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured Bubble Tea, No Milk, Less Sugar, No Topping is a freshly prepared fruit or tea-based beverage sold at Singaporean bubble tea outlets. This variant is customised with a reduced sugar level (typically 30–50% of standard sugar) and no tapioca pearls or other toppings, and contains no milk or dairy. The drink is made by combining a flavoured syrup or fruit concentrate with a tea base (such as green tea or black tea) and water, then shaken or blended with ice. The result is a lightly sweet, refreshing cold beverage with a clean tea or fruit flavour and no creamy or chewy elements. Per 100g, it provides 24 kcal, 6.21g carbohydrate (all as sugar), 0.01g protein, 0g fat, 0g dietary fibre, and 1mg sodium. The absence of milk and toppings significantly reduces calorie, fat, and carbohydrate content compared to standard bubble tea. Despite the 'less sugar' modification, the beverage still contains 6.21g sugar per 100g, qualifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage under ADA and AHA criteria. A standard 500ml serving delivers approximately 120 kcal and 31g of free sugar with no fibre buffer, resulting in rapid glycaemic absorption. This beverage is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease unless ordered at 0% sugar. It is vegan, dairy-free, gluten-free, and halal-suitable. Bubble tea is a culturally significant beverage originating from Taiwan and widely popular across Singapore and Southeast Asia.

How many calories in flavoured bubble tea less sugar?

Is boba tea without toppings lower in calories?
How much carbohydrate in bubble tea no milk no topping?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010025) with confidence 0.95 as reported in the input record. HPB is the primary approved source for Singaporean food records. All per-100g values are taken as reported without modification.
notes: Fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (D08010025); this is consistent with a tea/fruit-syrup-based beverage containing no milk or oil. Saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by the same source. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as this is a liquid beverage with no solid fruit pulp or fibre-containing ingredients in the less-sugar, no-topping formulation. Protein_g is 0.01g (trace), not zero. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these micronutrient and fatty acid fractions are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this item. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sugar-sweetened fruit-flavoured beverages; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in approved sources. Glycaemic load per 500g serving calculated as 62 × 31.05g / 100 ≈ 19.3. FoodOn match is approximate (FOODON_03311737, bubble tea); FoodEx2 code A0FKP (tea-based beverage) is approximate. No SNOMED CT code identified.
